items.name: Gemma 4 E4B
items.source_story_title: What's in a GGUF, besides the weights – and what's still missing?
items.summary: Dense multimodal local model suggested as a 4070-friendly pick.
items.why_included: A commenter explicitly told readers to try Gemma 4 E4B on a 4070.
items.evidence: In the GGUF thread, a commenter said Gemma 4 E4B should run great on a 4070 and put it forward as the better size match.

items.name: Qwen 3.6 35B A3B
items.source_story_title: What's in a GGUF, besides the weights – and what's still missing?
items.summary: Local MoE model recommended as the stronger big-run option.
items.why_included: The thread recommended it as a smarter local choice alongside LM Studio.
items.evidence: A commenter said Qwen 3.6 35B would be much smarter than 7B Mistral, just slower.

items.name: Lean 4
items.source_story_title: Saying Goodbye to one line of APL
items.summary: Functional language and theorem prover picked as an APL successor.
items.why_included: One commenter said they settled on Lean 4 as the successor language for their needs.
items.evidence: In the APL thread, a commenter called Lean 4 the 'best language in existence' for their use case.

items.name: Dyalog APL
items.source_story_title: Saying Goodbye to one line of APL
items.summary: Interactive APL implementation with full glyph support.
items.why_included: A commenter highlighted Dyalog APL as a usable modern APL environment.
items.evidence: A Raspberry Pi user noted Dyalog APL keeps all the glyphs visible and exposes a REPL.

items.name: YourDrive
items.source_story_title: Removing the modem and GPS from my 2024 RAV4 hybrid
items.why_included: A commenter said they used it and found it great and super affordable.
items.evidence: In the RAV4 privacy thread, a commenter praised YourDrive after using it a couple of times.

items.name: Red Balloon Security
items.summary: Embedded-device security firm and firmware-CTF shop.
items.why_included: A commenter explicitly recommended the firm to anyone interested in the challenge.
items.evidence: In the HDD firmware-hacking thread, a commenter called Red Balloon great and highly recommended it.
